Precision oncology of SPAtial immune esCape mEchanisms in ovarian cancerSPACE bi-weekly meeting
1Ziqi Kang Mar 14th, 2025

Meeting Agenda•Updates on the spatial analysis
•Metrics design and statistical tests
•HRP and HRD groups have diﬀerent tumor landscapes and spatial features
•But such features are not signiﬁcantly diﬀerent in other groups. 
•Cell interactions we want to focus?
•Updates about the deconvolution analysis. -> Whom can I seek help with when doing deconvolution analysis for bulk RNA-seq?

Tissue landscape patterns Myelonet + Lymphonet Immune hotspot

Spatial metrics design•Basic metrics: 
•# cell types and # RCN in each image
•Spatial metric - 1: Some tumor & stroma related metrics
•Tumor stroma ratio
•Tumor percentage •Tumor_stroma_interface_length: with max_dist = 50. 
•This metric measures the physical contact between tumor cells and stromal cells in an image, which reﬂects how much interaction occurs at the boundary between these 2 cell populations. Larger length -> more interaction that can indicate the shape of tumor or the inﬁltration of tumor.

Spatial metrics design: Tumor_stroma_interface_length

•Spatial metric - 2: Single cell type distribution metrics. For each cell type, 
•NND: nearest neighbour distance, measure the distribution of certain cell type, are they loose or dense. 
•cluster Density: compute the density of clusters with DBSCAN, radius = 30px, minimum of 10 cells are considered as a cluster. 
•Gini Density: Gini coeﬃcient for cell density variation, measure the inequality of a distribution. 
•More close to 1 means the more unequal the distribution of certain cell type. e.g. The unequal distribution of immune cells means there might be some immune structures like tertiary lymphoid nodes or immune networks. Spatial metrics design

•3: Spatial point analysis; —> Too time consuming, only on ROIs
•Instead, calculate the median distance of immune cells to TSI. Spatial metrics design•The ﬁnal goal is to see "How does the immune cells distributed around TSIs". 
•We need 1 -> What is the diﬀerence between the TSI RCNs and the interfaces identiﬁed by our own function? -> deﬁne the proper TSIs
•Use RCN.

Spatial metrics design•The ﬁnal goal is to see "How does the immune cells distributed around TSIs". 
•2 -> calculate the median (robust to extreme value) distance of each immune cell types to TSIs

Spatial metrics design•The ﬁnal goal is to see "How does the immune cells distributed around TSIs". 
•3 -> Statistical tests, show signiﬁcant diﬀerence among clinical groups
•4 -> TBD: plot the density trend of immune cell distribution with TSIs (tumor side and stromal side?)

Spatial metrics design•The ﬁnal goal is to see "How does the immune cells distributed around TSIs". 
•3 -> Statistical tests, show signiﬁcant diﬀerence among clinical groups
•4 -> TBD: plot the density trend of immune cell distribution with TSIs (tumor side and stromal side?)

•4: immune network, immune hotspots Spatial metrics design•Start with Delaunay triangulation. Question: choosing threshold for the longest edge of triangulation? The biological meaning? •I use 80px, 26um for now 
Myelonet

•4: immune network, immune hotspots Spatial metrics design•Start with Delaunay triangulation. Question: choosing threshold for the longest edge of triangulation? The biological meaning? •I use 80px for now 
Lymphonet I also included NK and Tregs.

•4: immune network, immune hotspots Spatial metrics design•Calculate the "Average edge length", "Clustering coeﬃcient", "Largest connected component size" and returns the graph objects. 
•Visualize the graph features of all images. 
•Perform statistical test across clinical groups.

Previous finding: Recurrent cellular neighbourhoods (RCNs) are enriched in different clinical groupsB-cell, Stromal-CAF and Stroma-residence immune niches are enriched in HRP group.

What did we learn from the results? •Time to take out a piece of paper!
•Supplementary materials: clinical info 
HRD: 52 (BRCA1/2: 11; BRCAwt: 41); 
HRP: 26; 
Unknown: 2
PFS long: 60;
PFS-short: 14; 
Remove: 6
Progression yes: 46;
no: 34

What kinds of cell interaction we want to explore?

•iMTB reports (WES) are ready. 
•Already ﬁnd bulk RNA-seq data, cleaned the TPM data matrix. 
•Successfully repeated the result of Kassandra analysis with example datasets —> •Next step: Train our Kassandra model for deconvolution task
•Who should I ask for help if further questions? 
Updates on WES and bulk RNA-seq analysis.

Plan for next step, other questions•How do I keep my mind clear while working on complex projects? 
•Can I start to make ﬁgures or write something? I feel more in progress if I end up making some nice ﬁgures or writing pseudomanuscript. 
😅
•Xiao Fu’s visit on April? What to prepare
•Doodle slots for my thesis committee meeting in April.

Let’s book the next meeting slotThanks for listening!
